title: xeraea nigricans
intro: Xeraea nigricans, also known as black sage, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Xeraea nigricans can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in topical applications, such as salves and creams.
